title: tecticornia indica subsp. ciliolata
intro: Tecticornia indica subsp. ciliolata, also known as the "Sea Saltbush", is a succulent plant native to Western Australia, characterized by its small, round leaves and ability to tolerate high salt concentrations. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Tecticornia indica subsp. ciliolata include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea or added to skincare products to harness its medicinal properties.
